item.title: Ambani set to fire starting gun on India’s largest IPO
item.description: Iran war has hit country’s equity market and disrupted what was positioned to be another record year for flotations

item.title: Ex-Citi executive says bank sacked her after raising Trump concerns
item.description: Former employee claims in lawsuit that she identified issues with risk-management practices

item.title: BMW cuts profit guidance as Iran war adds to challenges in Chinese market
item.description: Carmaker expects operating margin in its automotive division to be 1-3% in 2026, a sharp fall from previous projections

item.title: The White House’s capricious controls on Anthropic
item.description: An opaque approach to US policy risks hampering AI development

item.title: Australia-style approaches to social media ban may not work, Ofcom warns
item.description: Government considers putting age limits on virtual private networks to help enforce social media restrictions

item.title: GM and Lockheed Martin partner on boosting weapons production
item.description: Pentagon requested tie-up to help defence group boost its output of critical munitions

item.title: US and Europe discuss access to AI models after Anthropic dispute
item.description: ‘Trusted partner’ scheme would allow US allies to test cutting-edge models

item.title: Adobe paints a vivid picture of investors’ software pessimism
item.description: Maker of creative software is hardly in crisis but it does risk losing its edge

item.title: Russian warship accused of firing warning shots towards yacht in Channel
item.description: Ministry of Defence says it is ‘investigating reports of incident’

item.title: Thyssenkrupp to spin off materials trading business
item.description: German conglomerate pushes ahead with restructuring, but struggling steel business still has no buyer

item.title: Snap defies investor pressure with ‘Specs’ smart glasses launch
item.description: Chief Evan Spiegel described $2,195 eyewear as his ‘life’s work’ as he faces increasing criticism over costs of project

item.title: Whistleblower says JLR told him not to record alleged fire risks in Range Rover Evoque
item.description: Carmaker denies claims by former engineer who is seeking £3.7mn in employment tribunal case

item.title: ‘A referendum on oil and gas’: Britain’s other big by-election
item.description: Voters in Aberdeen go to the polls with both Conservative and Reform candidates making energy a central issue of their campaigns

item.title: Ministers seek to limit costs of next UK offshore wind round
item.description: Price will be ‘absolute central factor’ in upcoming auction as government fights to defend its clean power strategy

item.title: The best books on sport, health and wellness to read this summer
item.description: Simon Kuper and Anjana Ahuja select their best mid-year reads

item.title: Oil sinks below $80 a barrel as traders bet Strait of Hormuz flows will return
item.description: Brent crude has tumbled to three-month low following agreement to extend US-Iran ceasefire

item.title: France’s spy agency replaces Palantir with local rival
item.description: Move is part of European drive to reduce dependence on US tech firms

item.title: Yum Brands to offload Pizza Hut in $2.7bn deal
item.description: KFC and Taco Bell owner to sell bulk of fast-food chain to private equity group

item.title: Ericsson chief to step down after stabilising scandal-hit group
item.description: Börje Ekholm admitted possible payments by company to Isis in 2022 but then improved performance

item.title: EU resists banks’ plea for major overhaul of capital rules
item.description: European Banking Authority sets out ‘targeted’ plan to simplify overlapping requirements for lenders

item.title: Bunzl can create enough excitement to rebut Elliott’s proposals
item.description: FTSE 100 group looks like a poor candidate for the activist treatment

item.title: Who will pay for Canada’s proposed west coast pipeline?
item.description: The project aims to send up to 1mn barrels a day to Asia and reduce reliance on the US

item.title: SpaceX makes $60bn AI acquisition as market value challenges Amazon
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company extends blistering post-IPO rally

item.title: FirstFT: $300bn reconstruction fund to rebuild Iran
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Japan’s interest rates hit a 31-year high and private equity’s warning for law and accountancy

item.title: Environment secretary casts doubt on £10bn Thames Water rescue
item.description: Emma Reynolds’ warning increases prospect of the nationalisation of Britain’s biggest water company

item.title: India blocks Telegram in bid to halt exam fraud
item.description: Temporary ban introduced after reports that last month’s medical entrance test papers had been leaked

item.title: Spy agencies buy bulk data to assess potential targets
item.description: Commercially gained intelligence is now one of the major sources of government surveillance

item.title: Fujitsu chair quits after claims of ‘woman-related’ improper conduct
item.description: Hidenori Furata departs Japanese IT services group over allegations

item.title: Rathbones shares sink 18% as wealth manager halts investments by high-risk clients
item.description: Group at risk of near-£1bn hit to inflows as it pauses taking on clients who require ‘enhanced due diligence’

item.title: Jardine Matheson, titan of Asian commerce, pivots to developed markets
item.description: Chief executive Lincoln Pan says portfolio has become heavily skewed towards south-east Asia
